items.title: Fix credential holder assignment for superusers in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where superusers inadvertently became the holders of credentials when acting on behalf of others. The changes include a new audit field to track the actual creator of the credential and adjustments to the view logic to ensure the correct application owner is assigned as the holder. Additionally, regression tests have been added to cover various scenarios and ensure the integrity of the fix.

items.title: Enhanced Dialog Padding for Better UI in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update increases the horizontal padding of feature-flag dialogs to 48px, addressing cramped appearances on real screens. The dialog widths have also been adjusted: create/edit dialogs from 680px to 760px, scorecard from 860px to 920px, and evaluate from 520px to 560px. These changes improve the overall user experience without altering TypeScript functionality.

items.title: Enhance audio accessibility for mainland China users in Platform Service 🎶
items.summary: This update introduces a new helper function to mirror audio files from the upstream service to our COS CDN, ensuring users in mainland China can reliably access generated audio. The `transferFishAudioToCos` function downloads audio from the upstream URL and uploads it to our CDN, providing a stable link for users. This change does not affect request shapes, billing, or error handling.

items.title: Enhance Feature Flags and Scorecard Design in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a default enabled state for feature flags, improving admin usability. The dialog has been visually polished for better spacing and readability, while the scorecard has been redesigned to include significant statistical metrics, enhancing data interpretation for users. Additionally, internationalization support has been expanded with new keys and translations.

items.title: Kept Async Tool Polling Alive with Smarter Deduping in Platform Service ⚙️
items.summary: Updated the orchestrator and prompt guidance so async tool workflows continue polling until work actually completes instead of stopping after placeholder status messages. The duplicate-call detector now fingerprints tool output alongside inputs, allowing legitimate polling loops with changing results to proceed while still catching genuinely stuck calls. Added comprehensive tests covering repeated calls with evolving outputs and reinforced schema-agnostic handling so the system works across MCP and non-MCP tools alike.

items.title: Enhance Feature-Flag Admin UX in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the feature-flag admin page by addressing key UX issues identified during initial use. Improvements include adding an icon to the sidebar, reorganizing the header layout for better clarity, and enhancing the create/edit dialog with better spacing and guidance. Additionally, internationalization files have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring a consistent experience across languages.

items.title: Enhance Polling Metadata for Media MCPs in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces machine-readable polling envelope fields for 13 media MCPs, enabling orchestrators to reliably detect ongoing asynchronous generation. Key fields like `recommended_action`, `should_poll`, and `terminal_state_reached` have been added to improve task management without altering existing human-readable messages. This change is strictly additive, ensuring backward compatibility.

items.title: Superuser role filters now behave correctly in Backend 🛠️
items.summary: Fixed an applications query bug where superusers using role-based filters could unintentionally retrieve the entire applications table instead of scoped results. The update now honors `role=granted`, `role=owner`, and `include_granted` for all authenticated users while preserving unrestricted admin access when no role filter is provided. Added a dedicated regression test suite covering superuser and regular-user behavior to prevent future filter bypass issues.

items.title: Fix(aichat2): preserve pending_consent_request on resume so the resolved consent card renders in Platform Service 🐛
Black-screen QR capture fallback fixed in Wisdom 🖥️
items.summary: The automation capture pipeline now detects silent all-black screenshots from ImageGrab in headless or non-interactive RDP sessions and automatically falls back to the more reliable PrintWindow path. The update adds coarse-grid blank image detection, debug logging for fallback events, and expanded tests covering black frames, valid captures, and QR-like patterns. This resolves production login failures where QR codes rendered as black PNGs on freshly provisioned cloud VMs.

items.title: Enhance anti-narration rules for tool calls in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces explicit anti-narration clauses to RULE 3 and RULE 9, preventing the model from narrating tool calls instead of executing them. Key changes include forbidding specific phrases that delay consent and loader calls, ensuring a more direct interaction with users. The implementation has been validated with new tests, achieving a perfect pass rate.

items.title: Strengthens anti-hallucination guardrails for connector tool discovery in Platform Service 🛡️
items.summary: This update hardens RULE 9 to prevent models from inventing connector tool lists when users use prompts such as "without calling anything," "just tell me," or similar bypass framing. The connector instructions now explicitly state that tool catalogs exist only on the live server and require a loader call before enumeration. Test coverage was expanded with a new F-08 regression case, bringing the suite to 394 passing tests and reinforcing load-first behavior against fabricated responses.

items.title: Streamlined Authorization UI and Methods in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the legacy ApplicationAuthorization UI and associated operator methods as part of the credential-as-authorization cleanup. Key components, including the legacy authorize page and several outdated methods, have been eliminated, simplifying the codebase significantly. The changes enhance performance and maintainability by ensuring the new authorization path is always active in production.

items.title: Strengthen Anti-Paraphrase Rules in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the anti-paraphrase rules within the connectors block by adding three new clauses to improve model responses. Key changes include forbidding clarifying questions for unconnected connectors, specifying exact parameter shapes for loaders, and preventing the naming of unobserved tools. These adjustments aim to reduce hallucinations and ensure more accurate interactions across all models.

items.title: Fix management command for skill synchronization in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where the management command for syncing skills was incorrectly referenced, causing scheduled jobs to fail. The command has been updated from `sync_skill_catalog` to `sync_skills`, ensuring that the public Skill catalog is refreshed correctly. This fix will restore the functionality of the CronJob and allow new skills to be reflected in the `public_skills` table.

items.title: Retired Legacy ApplicationAuthorization Flow in Backend 🧹
items.summary: Completed the backend cleanup for the credential-as-authorization migration by removing the legacy ApplicationAuthorization model, API views, serializers, routes, and feature flags. Added a migration to safely drop the unused authorization table after confirming production no longer contains active rows. Credential creation logic was simplified by removing the old authorization lookup path, reducing backend complexity and eliminating dead configuration paths.
